scenarios.name: route-exact-match
scenarios.request: GET /health
scenarios.expected: 200, body 'ok' from the exact /health route

scenarios.name: route-prefix-match
scenarios.request: GET /static/app.js
scenarios.expected: 200 from the /static prefix route

scenarios.name: route-precedence
scenarios.request: GET /health (exact vs default)
scenarios.expected: exact route wins over the catch-all default (200 'ok', not 404)

scenarios.name: route-default-404
scenarios.request: GET /no-such-route
scenarios.expected: 404 default catch-all

scenarios.name: route-host-match
scenarios.request: GET /health/site with differing Host headers
scenarios.expected: host-discriminated routing (different Host -> different response)
scenarios.observed: Host a.example -> 200 b'vhost-a', Host b.example -> 200 b'vhost-b'
scenarios.note: deployed default handler (App.Handler.hostGlob) selects a virtual-host block by Host via the proven RouteAdvanced.dispatch

scenarios.name: route-glob-match
scenarios.request: GET /health/assets/img/logo.png (** glob route)
scenarios.expected: glob '**' suffix matching in the deployed route table
scenarios.note: deployed default handler carries a **-glob route matched by RouteAdvanced.matchPrefixSegs (the proven glob matcher)

scenarios.name: path-normalization
scenarios.request: GET /static/./app.js and //static//app.js
scenarios.expected: dot-segment + empty-segment normalization still matches /static (200)

scenarios.name: path-traversal-block
scenarios.request: GET /../../etc/passwd
scenarios.expected: 404 'traversal blocked' (escaping .. is refused, no file leaked)

scenarios.name: handler-respond
scenarios.request: GET /health
scenarios.expected: fixed 200 respond handler returns 'ok'

scenarios.name: handler-static-file
scenarios.request: GET /static/app.js
scenarios.expected: real file bytes from disk with ETag / Range / 304 support

scenarios.name: handler-redirect
scenarios.request: GET /old
scenarios.expected: 3xx redirect with a Location header

scenarios.name: handler-proxy-forward
scenarios.request: GET /api (reverse-proxy route to a live upstream)
scenarios.expected: request forwarded to a live backend over a real socket; the backend's body + X-Backend header returned
scenarios.note: the proven Reactor.ServeStep core yields proxyDial to the backend Reactor.ProxyDial.pick chose; the shell dials the live proxy_backend over a real socket (DRORB_EFFECT_SEAM=1 + DRORB_PROXY_BACKENDS)

scenarios.name: handler-cgi
scenarios.request: GET /cgi-bin/hello?a=1
scenarios.expected: CGI script executed (fork/execve), its stdout returned
scenarios.note: deployed .cgi route runs conformance/cgi-bin/hello via drorb_cgi_exec (launch the serve from the repo root so execve resolves the script path)

scenarios.name: mw-auth-jwt
scenarios.request: GET /admin with no bearer token
scenarios.expected: 401 Unauthorized with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer
scenarios.note: JWT gate runs the real Jwt.authenticate FSM, scoped to /admin*

scenarios.name: mw-auth-jwt-badtoken
scenarios.request: GET /admin with a garbage bearer token
scenarios.expected: 401 (signature/format fails the real validator)

scenarios.name: mw-auth-basic
scenarios.request: GET /private with no credentials
scenarios.expected: 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm on missing credentials
scenarios.note: BasicAuth gate (Reactor.Stage.BasicAuth.basicStage) composed into deployStagesFull2 as the second stage, scoped to /private*

scenarios.name: mw-cors-allow
scenarios.request: GET /health, Origin: https://app.example.com
scenarios.expected: Access-Control-Allow-Origin echoes the allowed origin

scenarios.name: mw-cors-deny
scenarios.request: GET /health, Origin: https://evil.example.com
scenarios.expected: no ACAO header for a disallowed origin

scenarios.name: mw-gzip
scenarios.request: GET /health, Accept-Encoding: gzip
scenarios.expected: Content-Encoding: gzip and a real gzip-compressed body

scenarios.name: mw-security-headers
scenarios.request: GET /health
scenarios.expected: HSTS + X-Frame-Options + X-Content-Type-Options + Referrer-Policy

scenarios.name: mw-ipfilter-block
scenarios.request: a request from a blocked CIDR (10.0.0.0/8)
scenarios.expected: 403 for a client address inside a denied CIDR block
scenarios.observed: forwarded client 10.0.0.1 -> 403 (body=b'forbidden: ip not admitted'); forwarded 127.0.0.1 -> 200 (admitted)
scenarios.note: the real peer address is threaded through drorb_serve_metered; IpFilter.permits deny-precedence over 10.0.0.0/8 fires on the real client address

scenarios.name: mw-ratelimit-429
scenarios.request: a burst that should trip the rate limit
scenarios.expected: 429 Too Many Requests once the token bucket empties
scenarios.observed: 8 x 200, 4 x 429 over one kept-alive connection (burst of 12)
scenarios.note: Rate.tryAdmit (cap 8) is driven by the real per-connection sequence threaded through drorb_serve_metered

scenarios.name: mw-header-rewrite
scenarios.request: GET /health (response header program)
scenarios.expected: hop-by-hop strip + Server field installed by the real Header.run rewrite
scenarios.note: Server install is observable; hop-by-hop stripping on a real forward is not exercised (no upstream connection)

scenarios.name: cache-hit
scenarios.request: two identical GET /static/app.js (second should HIT)
scenarios.expected: second request served from the cache with X-Cache: HIT + Age, body byte-identical, handler not re-run
scenarios.note: the proven ServeStep cacheable path yields cacheLookup/cacheStore for a gate-admitted GET /static (Cache.selectLifetime max-age=60); the shell's process-global store replays the stored bytes on the 2nd request (DRORB_EFFECT_SEAM=1)

scenarios.name: cache-coalesce
scenarios.request: 8 concurrent identical GET /static (coalescing)
scenarios.expected: duplicate concurrent misses collapsed onto ONE fetch: 1 leader MISS + K-1 waiters served X-Cache: HIT from that single fetch, all bodies identical
scenarios.observed: 8/8 answered 200; 7 carried X-Cache: HIT (expect 7); 1 distinct body
scenarios.note: the effect-seam cacheLookup elects one leader per key and collapses concurrent same-key misses behind its single fetch (Cache.coalesce_single_fetch); waiters are served the leader's bytes stamped X-Cache: HIT (DRORB_EFFECT_SEAM=1)

scenarios.name: proto-h1-keepalive
scenarios.request: 3 pipelined GET /health on ONE connection
scenarios.expected: all 3 answered 200 on the single kept-alive connection
scenarios.observed: 3 of 3 responses were 200 on one connection

scenarios.name: proto-h2c
scenarios.request: curl --http2-prior-knowledge GET /health
scenarios.expected: real H2 client completes an h2c GET (SETTINGS + HEADERS + DATA)
scenarios.note: input forks to the real H2 engine (serveIngress) but the socket-level H2 server does not drive a working SETTINGS/stream exchange

scenarios.name: proto-websocket
scenarios.request: RFC 6455 Upgrade + a masked text frame 'hi'
scenarios.expected: 101 Switching Protocols, correct Sec-WebSocket-Accept, and the frame echoed

scenarios.name: proto-quic-h3
scenarios.request: aioquic H3 GET /health over UDP
scenarios.expected: 1-RTT QUIC handshake + H3 200 'ok'

scenarios.name: proto-concurrent
scenarios.request: 24 concurrent GET /health connections
scenarios.expected: all concurrent requests answered 200 'ok'

scenarios.name: fabric-lb
scenarios.request: load balance across >=2 live upstream backends
scenarios.expected: distinct session keys distributed across >=2 live backends, each serving real traffic
scenarios.note: the proven Reactor.ProxyDial.pick (rendezvous hash over the eligible pool) chooses the backend; the seam keys affinity on the request target (the session id rides there), and the shell dials the live backend

scenarios.name: fabric-healthcheck
scenarios.request: eject an unhealthy backend from rotation
scenarios.expected: a killed backend is removed by active health checks; the key fails over to a live one
scenarios.observed: key 'hc' served by 'b0'; killed it; after health ejection served by 'b1' (status 200)
scenarios.note: the host's active-health loop TCP-probes each backend and clears the dead one's bit in the mask the proven Reactor.ProxyDial.pick consumes (health-eligible pool only)

scenarios.name: fabric-circuit-breaker
scenarios.request: short-circuit when no upstream is healthy
scenarios.expected: with the whole fleet down the proven pick finds no eligible backend and the core serves 503, dialling nothing
scenarios.note: no eligible backend (all bits clear via active-health ejection or an open breaker after consecutive forward failures) ⇒ Reactor.ProxyDial.pick returns none ⇒ ServeStep emits the proven serviceUnavailable503

scenarios.name: fabric-sticky
scenarios.request: sticky session affinity to one backend
scenarios.expected: the same session key is pinned to the same backend across requests
scenarios.note: the proven rendezvous pick is a pure function of the affinity key, so a repeated key resolves to the same backend; the seam keys affinity on the request target (SEED) — cookie-based sid affinity is the documented ServeStep.stickyKey follow-up

scenarios.name: comp-ipfilter-jwt-route-gzip
scenarios.request: GET /health, Accept-Encoding: gzip (ipfilter->jwt->route->gzip in one request)
scenarios.expected: admitted through ip-filter + jwt (off /admin) + route match, body gzipped
scenarios.note: the two gates are inert-but-present (pass-through); route + gzip do the work

scenarios.name: comp-proxy-headerrewrite-cors
scenarios.request: a forwarded request through proxy + header-rewrite + cors
scenarios.expected: FORWARD dimension only: a live backend answers 200 with X-Backend + body
scenarios.note: the proven ServeStep proxy path forwards to a live backend (DRORB_EFFECT_SEAM=1); hop-by-hop strip + CORS over the upstream reply is not yet in proxyRespTransform — a seam follow-up

scenarios.name: comp-cache-gzip
scenarios.request: a cached, gzip-compressed response
scenarios.expected: second request returns the cached compressed body (Content-Encoding: gzip) with X-Cache: HIT, without re-running the handler
scenarios.note: the effect seam stores the proven fold output — gzip stage included when Accept-Encoding requests it — and replays the compressed bytes on the 2nd request (DRORB_EFFECT_SEAM=1)

scenarios.name: comp-redirect-security
scenarios.request: GET /old (redirect + security-headers)
scenarios.expected: 3xx redirect that ALSO carries HSTS/security headers

scenarios.name: comp-ratelimit-keepalive
scenarios.request: 20-request burst on one kept-alive connection
scenarios.expected: some requests answered 429 once the bucket empties (rate-limit + keep-alive)
scenarios.note: keep-alive + the real per-connection rate bucket (cap 8): the burst depletes it and later requests are throttled

scenarios.name: comp-ws-after-auth
scenarios.request: WebSocket Upgrade on /admin (auth+WS)
scenarios.expected: the JWT gate refuses the unauthenticated WS upgrade (401), not 101
scenarios.note: the WS handshake is host-side framing, upstream of the JWT stage; auth does not compose with the upgrade path

scenarios.name: comp-h2-middleware
scenarios.request: an h2c request through the middleware stages
scenarios.expected: H2 request receives gzip/cors/security-header transforms
scenarios.note: the h2c serve routes the decoded request through the full 13-stage fold (deployRespFull2Of), so the security-headers/gzip transforms apply on H2

scenarios.name: comp-quic-route-middleware
scenarios.request: aioquic H3 GET /
scenarios.expected: route + middleware over H3

scenarios.name: native-jwt-gap
scenarios.request: GET /admin on orb-mac-multi (native TCP)
scenarios.expected: 401 (JWT gate), matching the dataplane
scenarios.observed: 401, body=b'invalid or missing bearer token' (403 policy, not 401 — no JWT stage on the native path)
scenarios.note: orb-mac-multi/IoMac run deployStepIngress = serveGuarded (3 stages); JWT is absent

scenarios.name: native-gzip-gap
scenarios.request: GET /health, Accept-Encoding: gzip on orb-mac-multi
scenarios.expected: Content-Encoding: gzip, matching the dataplane
scenarios.note: gzip stage absent from the native-socket guarded serve

scenarios.name: native-security-gap
scenarios.request: GET /health on orb-mac-multi
scenarios.expected: HSTS present, matching the dataplane
scenarios.note: security-headers stage absent from the native-socket guarded serve

scenarios.name: core-stdin-vs-socket
scenarios.request: GET /health via orb stdin AND via the dataplane socket
scenarios.expected: both binaries (same deployStepFull2) return an identical 200 'ok'

scenarios.name: braid-default-byte-identical
scenarios.request: braid ON (DRORB_BRAID=1) vs OFF on unmarked traffic
scenarios.expected: byte-identical response for every unmarked request (config-gated off)
scenarios.note: servePipelineBraided_off_eq: the composed stages are contextually transparent, so the fold is faithful to servePipelineFull2

scenarios.name: braid-forward-auth-denies
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Forward-Auth marker
scenarios.expected: 401 Unauthorized — the forward-auth gate short-circuits before the handler
scenarios.note: braided_fa_denies_status: pipeline_gate_status keeps the 401 through the whole inner onion

scenarios.name: braid-forward-auth-passes-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with NO forward-auth marker
scenarios.expected: 200 'ok' — the gate passes through an unmarked request

scenarios.name: braid-request-id-echo
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Request-Id: trace-abc-123
scenarios.expected: the response echoes X-Request-Id: trace-abc-123 (trusted id preserved)
scenarios.note: braided_rid_echoes_trusted: RequestId.resolve_trust_preserve, present in the finalized headers via the response onion

scenarios.name: braid-request-id-absent-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with no incoming id
scenarios.expected: no X-Request-Id header (the echo stage is a pass-through when unmarked)

scenarios.name: braid2-conn-limit-503
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Conn-Limit marker
scenarios.expected: 503 Service Unavailable — the per-source connection-cap gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: braided2_conn_denies_status (axioms {propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound})

scenarios.name: braid2-stick-table-429
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Stick-Limit marker
scenarios.expected: 429 Too Many Requests — the aggregated stick-table threshold gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: braided2_stick_denies_status (conn gate passes, then stick fires)

scenarios.name: braid2-slowloris-408
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Slow-Timeout marker
scenarios.expected: 408 Request Timeout — the slowloris header-timeout gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: braided2_slow_denies_status (conn+stick gates pass, then slow fires)

scenarios.name: braid2-error-page-404
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /no-such-route with X-Error-Page marker
scenarios.expected: 404 with the rendered custom error page as body (not the handler's default)
scenarios.note: braided2_errorpage_maps_404: ErrorPage.applyPage replaces the 404 body

scenarios.name: braid2-error-page-off-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /no-such-route with NO X-Error-Page marker
scenarios.expected: 404 with the handler's default body (the transform is a pass-through when unmarked)

scenarios.name: braid2-compress-content-encoding
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Compress-Ext + Accept-Encoding: zstd
scenarios.expected: Content-Encoding: zstd — the negotiated codec frames the body
scenarios.note: braided2_compress_encodes: CompressExt.content_encoding_set

scenarios.name: braid2-compress-off-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with Accept-Encoding: zstd but NO X-Compress-Ext marker
scenarios.expected: no zstd Content-Encoding (the compress transform is a pass-through when unmarked)

scenarios.name: braid3-conditional-304
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Conditional marker
scenarios.expected: 304 Not Modified — the RFC 7232 conditional gate short-circuits the handler
scenarios.note: braided3_conditional_304: the 304 survives the whole inner onion

scenarios.name: braid3-conditional-off-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with NO X-Conditional marker
scenarios.expected: 200 'ok' — the conditional gate passes an unmarked request

scenarios.name: braid3-variants-vary
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Variants marker
scenarios.expected: Vary: Accept-Encoding — the pre-compressed-variant representation-dependence header
scenarios.note: braided3_variants_vary: Variants.varyName/aeVary, variant_vary_always

scenarios.name: braid3-autoindex-listing
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with X-Autoindex marker
scenarios.expected: the response body is the real Autoindex directory listing (Index of /pub, one link row per entry)
scenarios.note: braided3_autoindex_lists: renderIndexHtml, autoindex_lists_dir/entry_in_listingRows

scenarios.name: braid3-autoindex-off-unmarked
scenarios.request: braid ON, GET /health with NO X-Autoindex marker
scenarios.expected: the handler's default body (the listing transform is a pass-through when unmarked)

scenarios.name: braid-dp-forward-auth-denies
scenarios.request: braided dataplane (DRORB_BRAID=1), GET /health with X-Forward-Auth marker
scenarios.expected: 401 Unauthorized FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the forward-auth gate short-circuits before the handler
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided_fa_denies_status: the 401 survives the metered inner onion

scenarios.name: braid-dp-request-id-echo
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Request-Id: dp-trace-777
scenarios.expected: the dataplane response echoes X-Request-Id: dp-trace-777
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided_rid_echoes: the trusted id is present in the finalized headers

scenarios.name: braid-dp-passes-unmarked
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with NO braid markers
scenarios.expected: 200 'ok' — the gate passes an unmarked request

scenarios.name: braid2-dp-conn-limit-503
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Conn-Limit marker
scenarios.expected: 503 FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the connection-cap gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided2_conn_denies_status

scenarios.name: braid2-dp-stick-table-429
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Stick-Limit marker
scenarios.expected: 429 FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the stick-table threshold gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided2_stick_denies_status

scenarios.name: braid2-dp-slowloris-408
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Slow-Timeout marker
scenarios.expected: 408 FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the slowloris timeout gate short-circuits
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided2_slow_denies_status

scenarios.name: braid2-dp-error-page-404
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /no-such-route with X-Error-Page marker
scenarios.expected: 404 with the rendered custom error page body FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE
scenarios.note: braided2_errorpage_maps_404 through the metered fold

scenarios.name: braid2-dp-compress-content-encoding
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Compress-Ext + Accept-Encoding: zstd
scenarios.expected: Content-Encoding: zstd FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided2_compress_encodes

scenarios.name: braid3-dp-conditional-304
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Conditional marker
scenarios.expected: 304 Not Modified FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the RFC 7232 conditional gate short-circuits with the real Cache.Conditional decision
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided3_conditional_304: the 304 survives the metered inner onion

scenarios.name: braid3-dp-variants-vary
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Variants marker
scenarios.expected: Vary: Accept-Encoding FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE — the pre-compressed-variant representation-dependence header
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided3_variants_vary: Variants.variant_vary_always

scenarios.name: braid3-dp-autoindex-listing
scenarios.request: braided dataplane, GET /health with X-Autoindex marker
scenarios.expected: the response body is the real Autoindex.renderIndexHtml directory listing (Index of /pub, one link row per entry) FROM THE RUST DATAPLANE
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided3_autoindex_lists: autoindex_lists_dir

scenarios.name: braid-dp-default-byte-identical
scenarios.request: braided dataplane vs default dataplane on unmarked traffic (live sockets)
scenarios.expected: byte-identical response for every unmarked request (config-gated off)
scenarios.note: servePipelineOfMetered_braided_off_eq: the composed head stages are transparent, so the metered fold is faithful to servePipelineFull2Metered
